description: Major UI/UX overhaul — replace emojis with Lucide icons, redesign layout to GitKraken-style three-column, implement SVG commit graph, add commit-level diff viewing, fix blink refresh, CSS modules migration, and full visual polish.

userStories.title: Install and Configure Lucide Icons
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Lucide icon library integrated so we can replace all emoji/unicode symbols with consistent, professional line icons.
acceptanceCriteria: lucide-react package installed as a dependency
One example icon renders correctly in the app (e.g., replace the settings gear emoji with Lucide Settings icon)
Icons accept size and className props for consistent sizing
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Foundation for all icon work. Just install and prove one icon works — do NOT replace all icons yet.

userStories.title: Replace Emoji Icons — Toolbar and Titlebar
userStories.description: As a user, I want professional Lucide icons in the toolbar and titlebar instead of tacky emojis.
acceptanceCriteria: All emoji/unicode icons in Toolbar.tsx replaced with Lucide icons: pull (ArrowDownToLine), push (ArrowUpFromLine), fetch (RefreshCw), stash (Archive), settings (Settings), branch (GitBranch), merge (GitMerge)
All emoji/unicode icons in TitleBar.tsx replaced: minimize (Minus), maximize (Square), close (X), logo/circle replaced with text wordmark or Lucide icon
Icons are 18px in toolbar, consistent spacing
No raw emoji or unicode symbols remain in Toolbar.tsx or TitleBar.tsx
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Focus ONLY on Toolbar.tsx and TitleBar.tsx. Other components will be handled in subsequent stories.

userStories.title: Replace Emoji Icons — Sidebar and Status Bar
userStories.description: As a user, I want professional Lucide icons in the sidebar and status bar.
acceptanceCriteria: All emoji/unicode icons in Sidebar.tsx replaced: collapse/expand chevrons (ChevronRight/ChevronDown), file icons (File/Folder), stash (Archive), branch (GitBranch), tag (Tag), remote (Globe)
All emoji/unicode icons in StatusBar.tsx replaced: notifications bell (Bell), refresh (RefreshCw), success/error (Check/AlertCircle), theme toggle (Sun/Moon), branch (GitBranch)
Icons are 14px in status bar, 16px inline in sidebar
No raw emoji or unicode symbols remain in Sidebar.tsx or StatusBar.tsx
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Focus ONLY on Sidebar.tsx and StatusBar.tsx.

userStories.title: Replace Emoji Icons — All Remaining Components
userStories.description: As a user, I want all remaining emoji/unicode symbols replaced with Lucide icons throughout the entire UI.
acceptanceCriteria: All emoji/unicode in NotificationToast.tsx replaced: info (Info), warning (AlertTriangle), error (AlertCircle), success (CheckCircle), close (X)
All emoji/unicode in StatusPanel.tsx replaced: file status icons (FilePlus, FileMinus, FileEdit), checkmarks (Check), warnings (AlertTriangle)
All emoji/unicode in CommitGraph.tsx replaced: any inline symbols
All emoji/unicode in WelcomeScreen.tsx replaced: open (FolderOpen), clone (GitBranch), init (FolderPlus), remove (X)
All emoji/unicode in remaining components (SearchPalette, SettingsPanel, CommitDialog, DiffViewer, BlameView, etc.) replaced
Grep for emoji/unicode patterns in src/renderer/ returns zero matches: no &#, no emoji codepoints, no raw unicode symbols used as icons
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Final emoji cleanup pass. After this, zero emoji/unicode icon symbols should remain in any renderer source file.

userStories.title: Create Design Token System
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a centralized design token CSS file so all spacing, typography, colors, and other values are consistent.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/renderer/src/styles/tokens.css with CSS custom properties organized into categories: spacing (--space-xs through --space-2xl), font sizes (--font-xs through --font-xl), border radius (--radius-sm/md/lg), shadows (--shadow-sm/md/lg), transitions (--transition-fast/normal)
Move all existing color variables from global.css :root into tokens.css
Both dark and light theme values defined in tokens.css via [data-theme] selectors
tokens.css imported at app root (App.tsx or main entry)
Existing app still renders correctly with no visual changes
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Create the token file and move color vars over. Do NOT split component CSS yet — that comes in subsequent stories.

userStories.title: CSS Modules — Toolbar, Titlebar, StatusBar
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Toolbar, TitleBar, and StatusBar styles extracted from global.css into CSS modules.
acceptanceCriteria: Create Toolbar.module.css with all toolbar-related styles extracted from global.css
Create TitleBar.module.css with all titlebar-related styles extracted from global.css
Create StatusBar.module.css with all status-bar-related styles extracted from global.css
Each component imports its own .module.css and uses scoped class names (styles.className pattern)
Hardcoded pixel values replaced with design tokens where applicable
Removed styles no longer present in global.css
No visual regressions — components look identical to before
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Extract styles for these 3 smaller components first. Update className references in TSX files.

userStories.title: CSS Modules — Sidebar
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Sidebar styles extracted from global.css into a CSS module.
acceptanceCriteria: Create Sidebar.module.css with all sidebar-related styles extracted from global.css (sidebar sections, branch lists, file tree, context menus, dialogs)
Sidebar.tsx imports Sidebar.module.css and uses scoped class names
Hardcoded pixel values replaced with design tokens where applicable
Removed styles no longer present in global.css
No visual regressions
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Sidebar is 2,272 lines with many style classes — this is a big extraction but scoped to one component.

userStories.title: CSS Modules — CommitGraph, StatusPanel, DiffViewer
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the CommitGraph, StatusPanel, and DiffViewer styles extracted from global.css into CSS modules.
acceptanceCriteria: Create CommitGraph.module.css with all commit-graph-related styles from global.css
Create StatusPanel.module.css with all status-panel-related styles from global.css
Create DiffViewer.module.css with all diff-viewer-related styles from global.css
Each component imports its own .module.css and uses scoped class names
Hardcoded pixel values replaced with design tokens where applicable
Removed styles no longer present in global.css
No visual regressions
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: These are the three largest remaining components with significant CSS.

userStories.title: CSS Modules — All Remaining Components and Cleanup
userStories.description: As a developer, I need all remaining component styles extracted from global.css into CSS modules, leaving global.css with only truly global styles.
acceptanceCriteria: CSS modules created for all remaining components: WelcomeScreen, SearchPalette, SettingsPanel, CommitDialog, NotificationToast, CodeEditor, BlameView, ConflictResolver, CommitFilterBar, KeyboardShortcutsPanel, and any others
global.css contains ONLY truly global styles: CSS reset, body/html, scrollbar styling, font-face declarations
No component-specific class names remain in global.css
All components use scoped class names from their .module.css
No visual regressions
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Final CSS cleanup pass. After this, global.css should be small (< 5KB) with only resets and global defaults.

userStories.title: Three-Column Layout — Structure
userStories.description: As a user, I want a GitKraken-style three-column layout so I can see branches, the commit graph, and details simultaneously.
acceptanceCriteria: AppLayout restructured into three resizable columns using react-resizable-panels: left sidebar (200-350px), center panel (flexible), right detail panel (250-500px)
Terminal panel remains at the bottom spanning full width below all three columns
All panel dividers are draggable with visible grab handles
Minimum sizes enforced: sidebar min 200px, center min 300px, right min 250px
Right panel collapses completely when no commit is selected (shows only sidebar + center)
Layout proportions persist across restarts via localStorage
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Set up the three-column structure. Don't implement hybrid overlay or icon rail yet — those come in later stories.

userStories.title: Sidebar Icon Rail and Collapse
userStories.description: As a user, I want to collapse the sidebar to an icon rail to save horizontal space.
acceptanceCriteria: Sidebar collapse/expand toggle button always visible at top of sidebar (use Lucide PanelLeftClose/PanelLeftOpen icon)
When collapsed, sidebar shows as a 48px wide icon rail with section icons vertically
Hovering an icon in rail mode shows a tooltip with the section name
Clicking an icon in rail mode expands that section as a floating overlay panel
Collapse state persists across restarts
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Depends on US-044a for the base layout structure.

userStories.title: Hybrid Right Detail Panel
userStories.description: As a user, I want the right detail panel to be an inline column on wide screens and a sliding overlay on narrow screens.
acceptanceCriteria: On window width >= 1400px: right detail panel renders as a dedicated inline column within the three-column layout
On window width < 1400px: right detail panel renders as a sliding overlay/drawer from the right edge, overlapping the center content
Overlay has a subtle backdrop shadow to visually separate from content beneath
Transition between inline and overlay modes is smooth when window is resized across the 1400px threshold
Panel can be dismissed via close button (X) or clicking outside the overlay
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Depends on US-044a. Use window resize observer to detect threshold.

userStories.title: Context-Aware Toolbar
userStories.description: As a user, I want toolbar buttons to only appear when they're relevant so I'm not confused by actions I can't perform.
acceptanceCriteria: When no repo is open: toolbar shows only Open Repository, Clone, and Init buttons
When a repo is open: toolbar shows git operation buttons (pull, push, fetch, branch, merge, stash)
Buttons that require a remote (push, pull, fetch) are hidden if repo has no remotes configured
Buttons show a disabled state with tooltip explaining why when preconditions aren't met (e.g., Nothing to push)
Active operations show a subtle inline spinner next to the button text, not replacing the button
Toolbar never wraps or overflows — excess buttons go into a ... overflow menu
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Depends on Lucide icons being in place (US-042a). Modifies Toolbar.tsx.

userStories.title: Fix Full-Screen Blink Refresh
userStories.description: As a user, I don't want the entire screen to flash white/blank when the app refreshes state.
acceptanceCriteria: Identify the cause of full-screen re-renders (likely top-level state change triggering full component tree remount, or file watcher causing complete repo reload)
State updates are granular — changing one piece of state (e.g., staging a file) does not re-render unrelated components (e.g., commit graph, sidebar)
File watcher updates are diffed against current state — only changed data triggers re-renders
No visible flash, flicker, or blank screen during any normal operation (stage, unstage, commit, checkout, fetch, pull, push)
Loading states use skeleton placeholders or spinners within their specific panel, never a blank screen
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Investigate state management in AppLayout/RepoView. Likely needs React.memo, useMemo, splitting state into independent atoms. May need to refactor how file watcher triggers refresh.

userStories.title: Sidebar Accessibility Fix
userStories.description: As a user, I want to always be able to access the sidebar content so I can switch branches, browse tags, and navigate the file tree.
acceptanceCriteria: Sidebar sections (Branches, Remotes, Tags, Stashes, Files) are always scrollable when content overflows
Collapsible sections use Lucide chevron icons with smooth rotation animation (90deg on expand)
Sidebar has its own scroll container independent of other panels
Keyboard accessible: Tab to focus sections, Enter to expand/collapse, arrow keys to navigate items
Sidebar collapse/expand toggle button is always visible (pinned to top of sidebar)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Fixes the current issue where sidebar content can become inaccessible. Depends on Lucide icons (US-042b) and sidebar CSS module (US-043c).

userStories.title: SVG Commit Graph — Lane Assignment Algorithm
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a lane assignment algorithm that determines which vertical column each branch occupies in the graph.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement a lane assignment algorithm that takes commit data (with parent hashes) and assigns each commit to a lane (column index)
Algorithm correctly handles: linear history (single lane), feature branches (new lane for divergence), merge commits (connecting lines between lanes), branch endings (lane freed for reuse)
HEAD and current branch assigned to lane 0 (leftmost)
Each branch gets a consistent color from a curated palette of 8-10 colors, cycling for additional branches
Algorithm exported as a pure function that can be unit tested
Unit tests cover: linear history, single branch, merge commit, multiple concurrent branches, octopus merge
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Pure data/logic — no rendering. This algorithm will be shared between SVG and Canvas renderers. Output: array of { commit, lane, color, parentConnections[] }.

userStories.title: SVG Commit Graph — SVG Renderer
userStories.description: As a user, I want a graphical commit history with colored branch lines and nodes rendered as SVG.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace the current ASCII-art commit graph with SVG rendering
Each commit is a colored circle node (8-10px radius) positioned on its assigned branch lane
Branch lanes are vertical colored lines connecting commits on the same branch
Merge commits show connecting bezier curve lines from the merged branch to the target branch
HEAD commit is visually distinct (larger node with highlighted ring or glow effect)
Branch and tag labels rendered as pill badges next to their commit node
Commit row still shows: short hash, message, author, relative date as HTML alongside the SVG
Virtualized rendering using react-window — only visible rows rendered in DOM
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Uses the lane assignment algorithm from US-048a. SVG for lines/nodes, HTML overlay for text. This replaces the existing CommitGraph rendering.

userStories.title: SVG Commit Graph — Canvas Fallback for Large Repos
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Canvas-based fallback renderer for repos with 50,000+ commits.
acceptanceCriteria: Canvas renderer produces visually identical output to the SVG renderer (same colors, node sizes, bezier curves)
Uses the same lane assignment algorithm from US-048a
Automatic switch from SVG to Canvas when commit count exceeds 50,000
Canvas renderer handles 100,000+ commits without performance degradation
Click/hover interactions still work on Canvas (hit-testing for commit nodes)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Canvas uses offscreen rendering for visible viewport + buffer. Hit-testing via coordinate mapping, not DOM events.

userStories.title: SVG Commit Graph — Interaction
userStories.description: As a user, I want to interact with the commit graph to select commits, view details, and perform actions.
acceptanceCriteria: Single-click a commit node or row to select it — selection highlighted with accent color
Selected commit's details populate the right detail panel
Right-click a commit for context menu: checkout, cherry-pick, revert, reset (soft/mixed/hard), create branch here, create tag here, copy SHA
Right-click a branch label for context menu: checkout, merge into current, rebase onto, delete, rename, push
Hover over a commit shows a tooltip with full commit message preview
Keyboard navigation: Up/Down arrows move selection, Enter opens commit details
Scroll position is preserved when data refreshes (no jumping to top)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Depends on SVG renderer (US-048b). Existing context menu logic can be adapted.

userStories.title: Commit Detail Panel
userStories.description: As a user, when I click a commit in the graph, I want to see its full details and changed files in the right panel.
acceptanceCriteria: Right panel shows when a commit is selected, auto-hides when deselected
Panel header: commit subject (bold), full SHA (copyable via click), author name + email, authored date (absolute + relative)
If commit is signed: show verification badge (Lucide ShieldCheck/ShieldAlert icon with status)
Full commit message body displayed below header
Changed Files section lists all files modified in the commit with Lucide status icons: FilePlus (added), FileMinus (deleted), FileEdit (modified), FileSymlink (renamed)
File count summary: e.g. 3 files changed, +42 -15
Click a file in the list to show its diff inline below
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Depends on three-column layout (US-044a) and graph interaction (US-049). Uses git:showCommit and git:showCommitFileDiff IPC handlers.

userStories.title: Integrated Diff Viewer for Commits
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see diffs for files in a commit so I can review what changed without leaving the app.
acceptanceCriteria: When a file is selected from the commit detail panel, its diff is rendered in the right panel below the file list
Supports inline (unified) and side-by-side modes toggled via buttons
Diff uses the existing DiffViewer component (word-level highlighting, syntax highlighting)
Diff header shows: file path, change type (added/modified/deleted/renamed), old path for renames
Binary files show Binary file changed message instead of diff content
Large diffs (1000+ lines) are collapsed by default with Expand button
Navigation between files: previous/next file buttons or keyboard shortcuts ([ and ])
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Depends on commit detail panel (US-050). Reuses existing DiffViewer component — this story wires it into the commit detail flow.

userStories.title: Redesigned Staging Area
userStories.description: As a user, I want a clear, well-organized staging area so I can review, stage, and unstage files before committing.
acceptanceCriteria: Staging area lives in the bottom portion of the center panel (below the graph), collapsible
Two-column file list: Unstaged Changes (left) and Staged Changes (right)
Each file shows: Lucide status icon, file name, directory path (muted text)
Stage a file: click + button or drag from unstaged to staged
Unstage a file: click - button or drag from staged to unstaged
Stage All and Unstage All buttons at the top of each column
Clicking a file in either column shows its diff in the right detail panel
Discard button (with confirmation dialog) on unstaged files
File counts shown in section headers: Unstaged Changes (5) / Staged Changes (2)
Commit message input and commit button at the bottom of the staged column
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Redesigns existing StatusPanel. Depends on three-column layout (US-044a) and Lucide icons.

userStories.title: Welcome Screen Redesign
userStories.description: As a user, I want a polished welcome screen when no repo is open so I can quickly get started.
acceptanceCriteria: Full-width centered layout with GitSlop wordmark text (not emoji logo)
Three action cards with Lucide icons: FolderOpen (Open Repository), GitBranch (Clone Repository), FolderPlus (Initialize Repository)
Recent Repositories list below actions showing: repo name (bold), full path (muted), last opened date (relative)
Hover on recent repo shows Remove from recent X button
Click a recent repo opens it immediately
If recent list is empty, show subtle No recent repositories message
Keyboard shortcut hints shown on action cards (e.g., Ctrl+O)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Redesigns existing WelcomeScreen component. Depends on Lucide icons.

userStories.title: Panel Resize and Collapse Animations
userStories.description: As a user, I want smooth transitions when panels resize or collapse so the UI feels polished.
acceptanceCriteria: Collapsing/expanding sidebar animates width over 200ms ease-out
Collapsing/expanding right detail panel animates width over 200ms ease-out
Collapsing/expanding terminal panel animates height over 200ms ease-out
Panel content fades in after expand completes (no layout flash during animation)
Dragging a panel divider is smooth with no jitter or lag
Double-clicking a divider resets that panel to its default size
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Polish story. May need CSS transitions on the panel wrapper elements and react-resizable-panels animation support.

userStories.title: Custom Context Menu Component
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a reusable custom context menu component with icons, shortcut hints, and consistent styling.
acceptanceCriteria: Create a reusable ContextMenu component that accepts menu items array with: label, icon (Lucide), shortcut hint, onClick, danger flag, separator flag
Menu renders at mouse position on right-click
Menu items show Lucide icon (left), label (center), keyboard shortcut hint (right, muted)
Dangerous actions (danger: true) are styled with red text and have a separator above them
Menu closes on: click outside, Escape key, or scroll
Menu repositions to stay within viewport bounds (doesn't clip off-screen)
ContextMenu.module.css with scoped styles using design tokens
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Reusable component — subsequent work will integrate it into commit graph, sidebar, and staging area.

userStories.title: Integrate Context Menus Throughout App
userStories.description: As a user, I want right-click context menus on commits, branches, files, tags, and stashes.
acceptanceCriteria: Context menu on commit graph nodes: checkout, cherry-pick, revert, reset (soft/mixed/hard), create branch, create tag, copy SHA
Context menu on branch labels (graph and sidebar): checkout, merge into current, rebase onto, delete, rename, push
Context menu on files in staging area: stage, unstage, discard, open in editor, copy path
Context menu on tags in sidebar: checkout, delete, push
Context menu on stashes in sidebar: apply, pop, drop
All context menus use the ContextMenu component from US-055a
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Depends on ContextMenu component (US-055a). Replace existing context menu implementations with the new component.

userStories.title: Loading States and Skeleton Screens
userStories.description: As a user, I want appropriate loading indicators instead of blank screens or stale data.
acceptanceCriteria: Initial repo load shows skeleton placeholders in each panel (not a blank screen)
Sidebar sections show skeleton list items while branches/tags load
Commit graph shows skeleton rows while history loads
Diff viewer shows a centered spinner while computing diff
Long operations (clone, fetch, push, pull) show progress in the status bar with percentage and operation name
Skeleton styles match the actual content dimensions (no layout shift when data loads)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Create a Skeleton component (pulsing gray rectangles) and integrate into panels.

userStories.title: Responsive Layout for Small Windows
userStories.description: As a user, I want the layout to adapt gracefully when I resize the window small.
acceptanceCriteria: At window width < 1200px: right detail panel auto-collapses to overlay mode
At window width < 900px: sidebar auto-collapses to icon rail mode
At window width < 700px: toolbar buttons collapse to icon-only (no text labels) and overflow to ... menu
Minimum window size enforced at 800x500 — no content is clipped or inaccessible below this
Panel min-width constraints prevent dragging panels to unusable sizes
Text truncates with ellipsis rather than wrapping or overflowing
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Depends on three-column layout (US-044a), icon rail (US-044b), and hybrid panel (US-044c). Uses window resize observer.

userStories.title: Notification System Redesign
userStories.description: As a user, I want unobtrusive notifications that don't disrupt my workflow.
acceptanceCriteria: Toast notifications appear in bottom-right corner, stacking upward (max 3 visible)
Each toast has: Lucide icon (color-coded by type), message text, dismiss X button
Toasts auto-dismiss after 4s (info/success) or 8s (warning/error)
Error toasts include a Show Details expandable section for stderr output
Notification bell in status bar shows unread count badge
Clicking the bell opens a notification history dropdown (last 50 items)
Toast entrance/exit animated with slide-in from right and fade-out
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Redesigns existing NotificationToast component and notification bell in StatusBar.

userStories.title: Titlebar and Status Bar Redesign
userStories.description: As a user, I want a clean titlebar and status bar that show useful information without clutter.
acceptanceCriteria: Titlebar: GitSlop wordmark text (left), current repo name + branch (center, clickable to copy), window controls with Lucide icons (right: Minus, Square/Copy, X)
Titlebar draggable area works for window movement
Status bar left section: current branch name with Lucide GitBranch icon, ahead/behind counts
Status bar center section: last operation status (e.g., Pushed to origin/main 2m ago)
Status bar right section: notification bell, encoding indicator, line ending indicator
Status bar text uses --font-xs size, muted until hovered
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Redesigns existing TitleBar and StatusBar components. Depends on Lucide icons and CSS modules being in place.
